zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Moore於1439年出生在英國越可爾比阿姆斯利時，她的父親Sir William Moore36歲，母親Thomasine Maleverer32歲。她於1470年與Sir George Hammerton在英國英格蘭亨廷格多史爾格德曼徹斯特結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世在英國英格蘭越可爾福拉比，埋葬在英國英格蘭越可爾福拉比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Moore于1439年出生在英国越可爾比阿姆斯利时，她的父亲Sir William Moore36岁，母亲Thomasine Maleverer32岁。她于1470年与Sir George Hammerton在英国英格兰亨廷格多史爾格德曼徹斯特结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世在英国英格兰越可爾福拉比，埋葬在英国英格兰越可爾福拉比。

nameMeanings.name: Moore
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Moor
meanings: English, Irish, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Moore  .
German: variant of   Mohr  .

Dutch (also De Moor): nickname for a man of swarthy complexion, from Middle Dutch moor ‘Moor’ (from Latin maurus), or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a Moor. Compare De Moor .

variants.name: More
meanings: French: nickname for a dark-skinned man, from a diminutive of Old French more ‘Moor’ (see More ).

variants.name: Morrin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Morin, a diminutive of More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus); see Moore 2 and 3.
English: perhaps sometimes from the Middle English personal name Morwin (Old English Mōrwine, the first element of which is uncertain and the second wine ‘friend’).

variants.name: Mauro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Domenic, Giuseppe, Antonio, Carmine, Carmelo, Domenico, Gaetano, Nino, Pasquale.

Italian (mainly southern): from the personal name Mauro, derived from the Latin personal and ethnic name Maurus, borne by various early Christian saints and meaning ‘Moor, North African’. Compare Moore and Moro .

variants.name: Moresco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Primo, Gino, Luciano, Riccardo.
Italian:
(Morésco): from a medieval personal name or a nickname for someone from North Africa or a swarthy or dark-haired man, from medieval Latin Mauriscus ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2).

variants.name: Morey
meanings: Irish (Limerick and Cork): variant of   Moore   4.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Mory, a pet form of Amauri (see Amery ).
English: perhaps also a variant of   Moore   1–3.

variants.name: Morrell
meanings: English (northern): from the medieval personal name Morel, a diminutive of Middle English or Old French More. Compare Moore .
Americanized form of French   Morel  . Compare   Morell   3.

variants.name: Moores
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): variant of Moore , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McMoore
meanings: Irish: variant of Moore 4, in which Mac- ‘son of’ has been prefixed in modern times to give the name (Ó Mórdha ‘descendant of Mórdha’) an unmistakably Gaelic identity.

variants.name: Moro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Mario, Alberto, Jorge, Lino, Luis, Luiz, Manuel, Ramiro, Adolfo, Aida.  Italian Angelo, Pino, Pio, Remo, Antonio, Ciro, Cosmo, Elio, Enrico, Geno, Giancarlo, Gino.
Spanish: nickname or ethnic name from moro ‘Moor’, from Latin Maurus ‘Moor, North African’.
Italian: nickname from moro ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2, compare 1 above), also ‘black-haired man’.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Moore was born in 1439, in Beamsley, Yorkshire, England, her father, Sir William Moore, was 36 and her mother, Thomasine Maleverer, was 32. She married Sir George Hammerton in 1470, in Godmanchester, Huntingdonshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died in Wragby, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Wragby, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Moore est née en 1439, à Beamsley, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Sir William Moore, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Thomasine Maleverer, avait 32 ans. Elle a épousé Sir George Hammerton en 1470, à Godmanchester, Huntingdonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée à Wragby, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterrée à Wragby, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Moore 1439 in Beamsley, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir William Moore 36 und ihre Mutter Thomasine Maleverer 32. Sie hat 1470 in Godmanchester, Huntingdonshire, England, United Kingdom Sir George Hammerton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb in Wragby, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Wragby, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Moore è nata il nel 1439 nel Beamsley, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, Sir William Moore, aveva 36 anni e sua madre, Thomasine Maleverer, aveva 32 anni. Lei sposato Sir George Hammerton nel 1470, nel Godmanchester, Huntingdonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel Wragby, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, e fu sepolta a Wragby, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Mooreが1439年にイギリスヨークシュアイアビームスリーで生まれた時，父Sir William Mooreは36歳，母Thomasine Malevererは32歳であった。1470年にイギリスイギリスハンティングドンシュアイアゴドマンチェスターでSir George Hammertonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女はイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアウラグビーで死去し，イギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアウラグビーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Moore(이)가 1439년 영국 요르크실르 브이슬리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William Moore(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Thomasine Maleverer(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 1470년 영국 잉글랜드 헌틴그도실르 고드먼체스터에서 Sir George Hammerton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 라그비에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 라그비에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Moore nasceu em 1439, em Beamsley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William Moore, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Thomasine Maleverer, tinha 32 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir George Hammerton em 1470, em Godmanchester, Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em Wragby, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, e foi sepultada em Wragby, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Moore родилась в 1439 г. в местности бимсли, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir William Moore было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Thomasine Maleverer, 32. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir George Hammerton в 1470 г. в местности годменчестир, хантингдоншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в местности рагби, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания и была похоронена в местности рагби, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Moore nació en 1439, en Beamsley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William Moore, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Thomasine Maleverer, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Sir George Hammerton en 1470, en Godmanchester, Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en Wragby, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, y fue sepultada en Wragby, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
